title: Mailchimp vs ConvertKit (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and ConvertKit email marketing platforms. Find the best fit for your business with pricing, features, and ratings.
h1: Mailchimp vs ConvertKit: Which Email Marketing Platform Wins?
intro: Mailchimp and ConvertKit are both popular email marketing platforms, but they cater to different audiences and use cases. Mailchimp targets small businesses and beginners seeking an all-in-one marketing solution, while ConvertKit focuses on creators, bloggers, and course sellers who need specialized automation. Choosing between them depends on your business model, budget, and specific feature requirements.

comparison_table.tool_a: Affordable pricing, drag-drop editor, landing pages, A/B testing
comparison_table.tool_b: Creator-focused features, visual automations, commerce integrations, subscriber tagging

choose_a_if: You're a complete beginner looking for an affordable, easy-to-use email marketing solution
You need competitive pricing with access to A/B testing and advanced segmentation
You want a platform that serves as an all-in-one marketing hub for general business needs
choose_b_if: You're a content creator, blogger, or course seller with specialized automation needs
You need built-in commerce features and subscriber tagging for personalized sequences
You're willing to invest more for a platform specifically designed for creator businesses

faq.a: Neither is objectively 'better'—it depends on your needs. Mailchimp excels for budget-conscious beginners and small businesses, while ConvertKit is superior for creators and course sellers. ConvertKit has higher customer satisfaction ratings on Trustpilot (3.2 vs 1.5), but Mailchimp offers better pricing and a gentler learning curve for newcomers.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or ConvertKit?
faq.a: Mailchimp is significantly cheaper, starting at $13/month compared to ConvertKit's $29/month. Additionally, Mailchimp's free tier supports 500 subscribers versus ConvertKit's 1,000, though ConvertKit's free plan offers more advanced creator-focused features despite the higher cost of paid plans.

faq.q: Can I switch from ConvertKit to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate your subscriber list from ConvertKit to Mailchimp. Both platforms support CSV exports and imports, making the transition straightforward. However, automation workflows and sequences won't transfer automatically, so you'll need to recreate those manually in your new platform.
